name: 311 Service Requests from 2010 to 2019
description:
311 responds to thousands of inquiries, comments and requests from customers every single day. This dataset represents only service requests that can be directed to specific agencies.  

This dataset is updated daily and expected values for many fields will change over time.  The lists of expected values associated with each column are not exhaustive.

Each row of data contains information about the service request, including complaint type, responding agency, and geographic location.  However the data does not reveal any personally identifying information about the customer who made the request.

For data from 2020-present see: https://data.cityofnewyork.us/Social-Services/311-Service-Requests-from-2020-to-Present/erm2-nwe9/about_data

columns.name: Unique Key
columns.description: Unique identifier of a Service Request (SR) in the open data set.

columns.name: Created Date
columns.description: The date and time that a Customer submits a Service Request.

columns.name: Closed Date
columns.description: The date and time that an Agency closes a Service Request.

columns.name: Agency
columns.description: Acronym of responding City Government Agency or entity responding to 311 Service Request.

columns.name: Agency Name
columns.description: Full agency name of responding City Government Agency, or entity responding to 311 service request.

Department of Health and Mental Hygiene
Senior Citizen Rent Increase Exemption Unit
Operations Unit - Department of Homeless Services

columns.name: Problem (formerly Complaint Type)
columns.description: This is the first level of a hierarchy identifying the topic of the incident or condition.

columns.name: Problem Detail (formerly Descriptor)
columns.description: A second level of detail about the Problem (formerly Complaint Type), and provides further detail on the incident or condition.

columns.name: Additional Details
columns.description: A third level of detail about the Problem (formerly Complaint Type) beyond the Problem Detail (formerly Descriptor).

top.item: NO HEAT AND NO HOT WATER

columns.name: Location Type
columns.description: Describes the type of location used in the address information

columns.name: Incident Zip
columns.description: Zip code of the incident address

columns.name: Incident Address
columns.description: House number and street name of incident address

columns.name: Street Name
columns.description: Street name of incident address

columns.name: Cross Street 1
columns.description: First Cross street based on the geo validated incident location.

columns.name: Cross Street 2
columns.description: Second Cross Street based on the geo validated incident location

columns.name: Intersection Street 1
columns.description: First intersecting street based on geo validated incident location

columns.name: Intersection Street 2
columns.description: Second intersecting street based on geo validated incident location

columns.name: Address Type
columns.description: Type of information available about the incident location.

columns.name: City
columns.description: Second intersecting street based on geo validated incident location

columns.name: Landmark
columns.description: If the incident location is identified as a Landmark the name of the landmark will display here

columns.name: Facility Type
columns.description: If applicable, this field describes the type of city facility associated to the service request

columns.name: Status
columns.description: Current status of the service request submitted

columns.name: Due Date
columns.description: Date when responding agency is expected to update the SR.  This is based on the Complaint Type and internal Service Level Agreements (SLAs).

columns.name: Resolution Description
columns.description: Describes the last action taken on the service request by the responding agency. May describe next or future steps.

cachedContents.largest: You were mailed a notice of complaint inactivation which detailed why this complaint was inactivated.  If you need more information, you may call (646) 632-6104

top.item: The Police Department responded to the complaint and with the information available observed no evidence of the violation at that time.
The Department of Housing Preservation and Development inspected the following conditions. No violations were issued. The complaint has been closed.
Service Request status for this request is available on the Department of Transportationâs website. Please click the âLearn Moreâ link below.
The Police Department responded to the complaint and took action to fix the condition.
The Department of Housing Preservation and Development inspected the following conditions. Violations were issued. Information about specific violations is available at www.nyc.gov/hpd.

The Department of Housing Preservation and Development was not able to gain access to inspect the following conditions. The complaint has been closed. If the condition still exists, please file a new complaint.
The Police Department responded and upon arrival those responsible for the condition were gone.
The Police Department responded to the complaint and determined that police action was not necessary.
The Department of Transportation inspected this complaint and repaired the problem.

The complaint you filed is a duplicate of a condition already reported by another tenant for a building-wide condition. The original complaint is still open. HPD may attempt to contact you to verify the correction of the condition or may conduct an inspection of your unit if the original complainant is not available for verification.

The Department of Housing Preservation and Development responded to a complaint of no heat or hot water and was advised by a tenant in the building that heat and hot water had been restored. If the condition still exists, please file a new complaint.
The Department of Buildings investigated this complaint and determined that no further action was necessary.
Please call 311 for further information. If you are outside of New York City, please call (212) NEW-YORK (212-639-9675).
The Police Department issued a summons in response to the complaint.

The Department of Housing Preservation and Development was not able to gain access to your apartment or others in the building to inspect for a lack of heat or hot water. The complaint has been closed. If the condition still exists, please file a new complaint.

The Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) didn't observe a violation of the NYC Air or Noise Code at the time of inspection and couldn't issue a summons.  If the problem still exists, please go to nyc.gov/311 or call 311 to file a new Service Request. If you're outside of New York City, please call (212) NEW-YORK (212-639-9675).
The Department of Sanitation mailed the literature. Please allow one week for delivery.
The Department of Sanitation investigated this complaint and found no violation at the location.
The Police Department reviewed your complaint and provided additional information below.

cachedContents.smallest: A copy of the requested document was mailed.

columns.name: Resolution Action Updated Date
columns.description: Date when responding agency last updated the service request.

columns.name: Community Board
columns.description: Community boards are local representative bodies. There are 59 community boards throughout the City, each representing a distinct geography.

columns.name: Council District
columns.description: The City Council district where the service request is located.

columns.name: Police Precinct
columns.description: The NYPD precinct where the service request is located

columns.name: BBL
columns.description: Parcel number that identifies the location of the building or property associated with the service request

columns.name: Borough
columns.description: One of New York City's five boroughs

columns.name: X Coordinate (State Plane)
columns.description: Geo validated,  Y coordinate of the incident location.

columns.name: Y Coordinate (State Plane)
columns.description: Geo validated,  Y coordinate of the incident location.

columns.name: Open Data Channel Type
columns.description: Indicates how the service request was submitted to 311.

columns.name: Park Facility Name
columns.description: If the incident location is a Parks Dept facility and service requests pertains to a facility managed by NYC Parks (DPR), the name of the facility will appear here

columns.name: Park Borough
columns.description: The borough of incident if the service request is pertaining to a NYC Parks Dept facility (DPR)

columns.name: Vehicle Type
columns.description: "Data provided if service request pertains to a vehicle managed by the Taxi and Limousine Commision (TLC).  "

columns.name: Taxi Company Borough
columns.description: Data provided if service request pertains to a vehicle managed by the Taxi and Limousine Commision (TLC).

columns.name: Taxi Pick Up Location
columns.description: If the incident pertains a vehicle managed by the Taxi and Limousine Commision (TLC), this field displays the taxi pick up location

columns.name: Bridge Highway Name
columns.description: "If the incident is identified as a Bridge/Highway, the name will be displayed here.  "

columns.name: Bridge Highway Direction
columns.description: If the incident is identified as a Bridge/Highway, the direction where the issue took place would be displayed here.

columns.name: Road Ramp
columns.description: If the incident location was Bridge/Highway this column differentiates if the issue was on the Road or the Ramp.

columns.name: Bridge Highway Segment
columns.description: Additional information on the section of the Bridge/Highway were the incident took place.

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: Geo based Latitude of the incident location in decimal degrees.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: Geo based Longitude of the incident location in decimal degrees.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: The combined geo-based latitude & longitude of the incident location.

columns.name: Community Districts
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Community Districts' (f5dn-yrer) the point in column 'location' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: Borough Boundaries
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Borough Boundaries' (yeji-bk3q) the point in column 'location' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: Police Precincts
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Police Precincts' (sbqj-enih) the point in column 'location' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: City Council Districts
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'City Council Districts' (92fq-4b7q) the point in column 'location' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.
